data.body: B4 parity gaps surfaced by B3's code review (close these in the parity harness): (1) the workflow completion-verification drops an agent-supplied 'summary' (the closed expression grammar can't reference an optional/possibly-absent step output without raising; B3 wires only always-present outputs) — completion.py carries summary when present, so the parity harness must either make the verifier always emit summary or special-case it; (2) epic graph target: completion.py defaults graph=True for epics and sets target.ticket_ids from _assemble_context (may include descendants), whereas the workflow uses [canonical_id]; (3) the stock FakeAgentRunner (mode=structured -> {result:{}}) does not produce a verdict, so the gate needs a real/canned runner — the parity corpus must inject one.
